g.text: 3. CONTENT-ADDRESSED STORAGE (IPFS, BitTorrent v2, Tahoe-LAFS, Perkeep)

text.tspan: Two replicas should hold the same data but might have drifted.
Each computes a Merkle tree of its data (partitioned by token range).
Exchange root hashes u2192 differ?
Descend into mismatched subtree u2192 find specific divergent rows.
Repair only the differences (not the whole dataset).
u2192 Cassandra calls this 'nodetool repair'
Bitcoin: each block's header includes a Merkle root over all transactions.
u2192 SPV clients verify a transaction was included with O(log N) proof
u2192 alteration of ANY transaction changes block hash u2192 detected by consensus
Git: every commit's tree object IS a Merkle tree of the file content.
u2192 commit hash uniquely identifies the entire repo state
u2192 'git fsck' uses Merkle tree to verify object integrity
Files are split into chunks; each chunk's hash IS its address.
The root of the chunk Merkle tree identifies the whole file.
u2192 immutable: changing a chunk gives a different hash u2192 different file
u2192 deduplication: identical chunks across files share storage
u2192 integrity: download is verifiable; corruption is detectable
Public log of every TLS certificate ever issued (so rogue certs can be detected).
Merkle tree structure provides: (a) tamper-evidence (any log change is visible),
(b) cryptographic 'inclusion proofs' u2014 a short proof that cert X is in log of N total.
